title: Listening: Ordering at a Restaurant
learningGoal: Understand spoken requests using the 'Chahiye' construction and adjective modifiers.
instructions: Listen to the conversation between a waiter and a customer. Notice how the customer uses 'Mujhe' and 'Chahiye' to order, and how adjectives are used to specify exactly what kind of food and drink they want.
listeningGoal: Identify dative pronouns and adjective-noun pairings in spoken Hindi.
audioScenario: A customer is ordering food and drinks at a restaurant.

audioTranscriptSentences.targetText: वेटर: नमस्ते सर। आपको क्या चाहिए?
audioTranscriptSentences.transliteration: Waiter: Namaste Sir. Aapko kya chahiye?
audioTranscriptSentences.baseTranslation: Waiter: Hello Sir. What do you want?

audioTranscriptSentences.targetText: वेटर: गरम चाय या ठंडी चाय?
audioTranscriptSentences.transliteration: Waiter: Garam chai ya thandi chai?
audioTranscriptSentences.baseTranslation: Waiter: Hot tea or cold tea?

audioTranscriptSentences.targetText: वेटर: ठीक है। और खाना? आपको क्या चाहिए?
audioTranscriptSentences.transliteration: Waiter: Theek hai. Aur khaana? Aapko kya chahiye?
audioTranscriptSentences.baseTranslation: Waiter: Okay. And food? What do you want?

audioTranscriptSentences.targetText: ग्राहक: मुझे खाना नहीं चाहिए। धन्यवाद।
audioTranscriptSentences.transliteration: Grahak: Mujhe khaana nahi chahiye. Dhanyawad.
audioTranscriptSentences.baseTranslation: Customer: I do not want food. Thank you.

audioTranscriptFull: नमस्ते सर। आपको क्या चाहिए? नमस्ते। मुझे चाय चाहिए। गरम चाय या ठंडी चाय? मुझे गरम चाय चाहिए। ठीक है। और खाना? आपको क्या चाहिए? मुझे खाना नहीं चाहिए। धन्यवाद।

questionSet.questionText: How does the waiter ask 'What do you want?'
What does the customer order first?
What does the word 'ya' (या) mean in the sentence 'Garam chai ya thandi chai'?
What type of tea does the customer specifically request?
What does the customer say when the waiter asks about food?
options: Mujhe khaana nahi chahiye (I don't want food)
Khaana kharaab hai (The food is bad)

correctAnswers.lq_5: Mujhe khaana nahi chahiye (I don't want food)

feedbackRules.onPass: Great listening! You successfully tracked the 'Chahiye' structure.
feedbackRules.onFail: Listen to the pronouns carefully. 'Aapko' means 'To you'. 'Mujhe' means 'To me'.
feedbackRules.onPartial: Good effort. Note that 'ya' means 'or', while 'aur' means 'and'.

audioAssets.audioTranscript: नमस्ते सर। आपको क्या चाहिए? नमस्ते। मुझे चाय चाहिए। गरम चाय या ठंडी चाय? मुझे गरम चाय चाहिए। ठीक है। और खाना? आपको क्या चाहिए? मुझे खाना नहीं चाहिए। धन्यवाद।

hintText: Notice how you say 'I do not want'. The word 'nahi' (no/not) comes right before 'chahiye'.
